findings.evidence: `rg -n "LiftCondition|liftCondition_iff_range_le"` shows `@[expose] def LiftCondition` at line 59 and an `Iff.rfl` restatement at lines 66-71.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]`; keep the body opaque and make the characterization lemma the public way to rewrite/use the condition, adding automation there if desired.
findings.issue: `LiftCondition` is marked `@[expose]`, exposing the body even though line 66 already provides the range-inclusion characterization.

findings.evidence: Roadmap lines 100-106 ask to consume `IsCoveringMap.existsUnique_continuousMap_lifts_of_range_le` and note the simply-connected case; Mathlib has those theorems at `Mathlib/Topology/Homotopy/Lifting.lean:469` and `:490`, while this file only defines the predicate and iff.
findings.fix: Add theorem wrappers such as `hp.existsUnique_continuousMap_lifts_of_liftCondition he hcond` and the simply-connected `hp.existsUnique_continuousMap_lifts ...`, so consumers can apply the criterion without restating the raw subgroup inclusion.
findings.issue: The API stops at `liftCondition_iff_range_le`; it does not state the actual lifting criterion using `LiftCondition`, nor the simply-connected specialization noted by the roadmap.

summary: The public API exposes the predicate body and does not provide the characteristic lifting theorems needed by the roadmap target. Downstream users still have to unfold/translate the condition into Mathlib’s raw hypothesis instead of using a TauCeti lifting-criterion API.
